task: Compare the Acura CL 2003 with the ILX 2022.
graderReasoning: The agent completed the task on Cars.com by opening the compare tool, selecting Acura CL 2003 as the first car and Acura ILX 2022 as the second car, and reaching the comparison results page titled 'Acura CL vs. Acura ILX'. The final page visibly showed the selected trims ('2003 Acura CL' with '2dr Cpe 3.2L' and '2022 Acura ILX' with 'Sedan') and comparison data including MSRP, consumer rating, engine type, MPG, horsepower, seating, and warranty. Additional expanded sections provided torque, transmission, and trunk volume, supporting the summary the agent gave. The extracted comparison details are backed by the page snapshots and are consistent with the visible Cars.com data.
final_result_response:
Done. I opened Cars.com’s compare tool and selected:

- 2003 Acura CL, 2dr Cpe 3.2L
- 2022 Acura ILX, Sedan

Quick comparison from Cars.com:

| Category | 2003 Acura CL | 2022 Acura ILX |
|---|---:|---:|
| Starting MSRP | $28,200 | $27,300 |
| Consumer rating | 4.6 | 5.0 |
| Engine | Gas V6 | Premium Unleaded I-4 |
| Horsepower | 225 hp | 201 hp |
| Torque | 216 @ 4700 | 180 @ 3600 |
| MPG | 20 city / 29 hwy | 24 city / 34 hwy |
| Transmission | 5-speed automatic | 8-speed automatic |
| Drive type | Front-wheel drive | Front-wheel drive |
| Seating | 4 | 5 |
| Trunk volume | 14 ft³ | 12 ft³ |
| Basic warranty | 4 yr / 50,000 mi | 4 yr / 50,000 mi |

Bottom line: the 2003 CL has more power and a larger V6, while the 2022 ILX is more fuel-efficient, seats one more person, and has the higher Cars.com consumer rating.
action_history: Opening Cars.com: const p1 = await openTab('https://www.cars.com/');
Expanding key comparison sections: await btn.click().catch(async err => console.log('click failed '+name+': '+err.message));
